title: christella malabariensis
intro: Christella malabariensis is a type of fern plant, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Christella malabariensis include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating various skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. Christella malabariensis is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or paste, and can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tonics, and creams, to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
